description: Email sequence for PISCO outreach to Spanish hotels

emails.title: Director RRHH
emails.subject: Quick question about NH's staffing costs
emails.body_intro: I'm Jordan, IE MBA student working on hospitality staffing.
emails.key_message: I analyzed the true cost of hiring for Spanish hotels. For a 200-room property: $193,100 in true costs.
emails.body:
Hi Javier,

I'm Jordan — IE MBA student, working with Spanish hotels on staffing strategy.

I recently analyzed the true cost of hospitality hiring in Spain. For a 200-room property hiring 5 workers:

- Stated cost: ~$80,000/year
- True cost (including failure, turnover, training): $193,100/year

Most properties don't see the full picture until they're already 3 hires deep.

Would you be open to a 15-minute call to walk through the numbers for NH?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79
emails.notes: Javier controls hiring for ALL NH Spain properties. ONE YES = massive.

emails.title: Human Resources Director
emails.subject: Summer content for NH Collection Madrid?
emails.body:
Hi Miriam,

I'm Jordan — IE MBA student based in Madrid.

I noticed NH Collection Madrid Abascal is likely preparing for summer season staffing.

I'm building an outreach service for hotels — we identify the true cost of staffing and present solutions that actually work.

For your property specifically: 5 hires through a specialized agency = $193,100 true cost vs $291,000 industry standard.

Would you be open to a quick call to see if this could help NH Collection Madrid this summer?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79
emails.notes: Local Madrid property. Walking distance if needed. Summer staffing crisis.

emails.title: Room Mate
emails.subject: Quick update from Jordan — staffing research
emails.body:
Hi Yolanda,

Quick update from Jordan — IE MBA student, Madrid.

I've been building out the PISCO research and wanted to share something interesting:

Room Mate's staffing approach with PISCO: 8 positions filled in 19 days, 18 months zero turnover, €47,000 annual savings.

I'm now offering an outreach service for hotels — essentially doing for other properties what we did for Room Mate.

Would you be open to a 10-minute call this week? I can share what we found.

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79
emails.notes: Already familiar with PISCO. Warm lead. May refer or extend.

emails.title: Human Resources
emails.subject: Quick question about Iberostar's staffing approach
emails.body:
Hi Luis,

I'm Jordan — IE MBA student working on hospitality staffing solutions in Spain.

Iberostar has a strong presence across Spain with diverse properties. I'm curious: what's your staffing situation heading into peak season?

I recently completed research on the true cost of hospitality hiring. For a 200-room property:

- Industry standard cost: $291,000/year (including failure/turnover)
- With specialized recruitment: $193,100/year
- Savings: $97,900 per property

Would you have 15 minutes to discuss?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79
emails.notes: Large chain. Multiple properties. High potential if one division says yes.

emails.title: HR Director
emails.subject: Quick question about Meliá's staffing strategy
emails.body:
Hi María,

I'm Jordan — IE MBA student researching hospitality staffing in Spain.

Meliá has an incredible footprint across Spain and internationally. I'm curious: how is the staffing situation affecting your properties this year?

I just finished a deep analysis on true staffing costs. For a typical 200-room property:

- True annual cost with standard recruitment: $291,000
- With specialized Filipino recruitment: $193,100
- That difference: $97,900/year per property

Would a 15-minute call make sense?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79
emails.notes: Major chain. All properties could use this. High value.

emails.title: Human Resources Manager

I'm Jordan — IE MBA student working with Spanish hotels on staffing.

I analyzed the true cost of hospitality hiring. For a luxury property of your size:

- Industry standard: $465,600

Would you have 15 minutes for a call?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79
emails.notes: Luxury property = higher savings. Barcelona market is strong.

emails.title: RRHH Manager
emails.subject: Staffing question for NH Figueres
emails.body:
Hi Montserrat,

I'm Jordan — IE MBA student working on hospitality staffing.

NH Figueres is in a unique market near the French border. I'm curious: what staffing challenges are you facing?

I completed research on true hiring costs:

Small team, but the savings are still significant.

Would a brief call make sense?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79

emails.title: Room Mate
emails.subject: Following up from Room Mate success
emails.body:
Hi Carlos,

I'm Jordan — following up on the PISCO outreach we've done with Room Mate properties.

The results have been strong: 8 positions, 19 days, 18 months zero turnover, €47,000 savings.

I'm now reaching out to other properties to see if there's interest in a similar approach.

Would you have 10 minutes for a quick conversation?

Best,
Jordan
+34 612 22 38 79
emails.notes: Warm lead from Room Mate success. Should be easier to convert.

first_follow_up.subject: Re: {original_subject}
first_follow_up.body:
Hi {name},

Following up on my note from {days_ago} about staffing costs for {company}.

I know you're busy — completely understand if this isn't the right time.

But if it is: the timing matters. Summer season planning is happening now.

Happy to send over a quick breakdown for {company} specifically. Just say the word.

Best,
Jordan

second_follow_up.subject: Re: Re: {original_subject}
second_follow_up.body:
Hi {name},

One more follow-up — then I'll leave you alone.

I analyzed {company}'s specific situation. Here's the bottom line:

{estimated_savings} in annual savings on staffing costs.

That's not small talk. That's real money.

If you're even slightly curious: I'd love to show you the numbers.

Best,
Jordan

final_follow_up.subject: One last thing
final_follow_up.body:
Hi {name},

I've stopped following up after this.

But I wanted to leave you with one thought:

The hotels that solve their staffing problem this year will have a significant advantage. The ones that don't will be fighting for the same limited pool of workers.

If you want to chat about it someday, I'm here. If not: good luck with the season.

Best,
Jordan
